rule: raw:m5-ordinal-infra-implementation-final-form-and-limits-omega-cycle (parent raw 47 + raw 70 inherited; subject narrowed to ordinal-infra build)
subject: m5 ordinal infrastructure module — CNF parser/comparator + schema migration of state/meta_engine_evolution_log.jsonl (R4..R41, 23 rows) + Kleene fixpoint detector (3-step plateau window) + back-stamp migrator + ordinal-budget tracker (per-engine ε_0 vs Γ_0 ceiling). Subject of THIS ω-cycle is the IMPLEMENTATION-PLANE of m5, not m5 axis itself.
purpose: Before any code is written for m5, enumerate (Track-A) the final-form requirements that the ordinal-infra module must satisfy if it is to actually serve as the escape anchor of m11/m12/m17/m18, and (Track-B) the physical/mathematical limits that constrain or falsify the implementation itself (parser ambiguity, undecidability beyond Γ_0, retroactive labeling = post-hoc rationalization). Goal: refuse to ship code until the limit-axis falsifiers are explicitly bound to test corpora. m5 is prerequisite for m3, m6, m9, m10 — all four are blocked until ordinal stamps land.
trigger: user 2026-04-26 'm5 ordinal infrastructure implementation 에 대해 ω-cycle 발사 — code 는 쓰지 말고 witness 만'.
method: two-track multi-axis ideation, scoped to the m5 implementation surface. Track-A (final-form, 7 axes) = what the ordinal-infra module must do to actually deliver the m5 contract (CNF correctness, Veblen φ extension, retroactive schema migration, comparator unit-test corpus, fixpoint window-detector, historical 23-round migration, ordinal-budget tracker). Track-B (limits, 7 axes) = what mathematics / physics / reasoning hygiene forbid even an ideal implementation from doing (undecidability >Γ_0, parser ambiguity for non-canonical CNF, m18 ordinal exhaustion of meta system itself, m17 Kleene self-fixpoint, Cantor-Bendixson rank uncomputable, non-elementary cost of φ_α(0), retroactive-labeling = post-hoc rationalization). dedup → cluster → Tier. omega_stop = enumeration fixpoint + each Tier-1 carries (loc_estimate, theory_anchor, falsifier).
ordinal_self_stamp_basis: ATR_0 (Feferman-Schütte). Inherited from m5 dossier §Appendix — m5 is the framework axis classifying ε_0-level ordinals, so its own infra must sit at the predicative ceiling Γ_0. THIS witness inherits m5's stamp because it is the implementation-plane of m5 (one ordinal level below the parent ω-cycle's Γ_0 ceiling, but at the same Γ_0 framework anchor).
current_state_pre_impl: state/meta_engine_evolution_log.jsonl — 23 rows R4..R41 with `round:int` only; no ordinal_label / predecessor / strict_descent / escape_basis fields
no parser, no comparator, no fixpoint detector exists in tool/
design/hexa_sim/falsifiers.jsonl — falsifier substrate exists (m6 pattern target)
m3 / m6 / m9 / m10 — Tier-1 backlog from parent ω-cycle, all blocked on m5
23 historical rounds need back-stamps (R5→R7→R10... claim closed-loop progression)
known_blockers_inherited: AG10_tool_write — does NOT block m5 (m5 emits state/jsonl + tool/ python module, not agent-mediated tool synthesis)
m18 ordinal_exhaustion_PA — bounds ceiling of meta system itself (Γ_0 wall on impl)
m17 Kleene recursion — implementation itself can fall into self-fixpoint (3-plateau on ordinal stamp)

axes_surfaced.name: cnf_parser_correctness
axes_surfaced.summary: parse_cnf(s) for α < ε_0 must be a total function on the canonical-CNF input grammar `α ::= 0 | n | ω^β·c (+ω^β·c)* | ε_0 | Γ_0`, with sentinel handling for ε_0/Γ_0/ψ(Ω_ω). Tokenisation via regex over {ω, ^, ·, +, digits, ε_0, Γ_0}; recursion on exponents. Reject input outside CNF grammar (raise ParseError → F-m5-B falsifier event). 25 LoC core.

axes_surfaced.name: cnf_comparator_correctness
axes_surfaced.summary: cmp_cnf(α, β) ∈ {-1, 0, +1} satisfying trichotomy + transitivity + agreement with hand-checked corpus of ≥50 ordinal pairs spanning 0, n, ω, ω+k, ω·k, ω^k, ω^ω, ε_0, Γ_0. Lex on (leading_exponent, leading_coefficient, tail). 12 LoC core. Test corpus committed as fixture.

axes_surfaced.name: veblen_phi_sentinel_extension
axes_surfaced.summary: For ordinals at or beyond ε_0, parser does NOT compute Veblen φ-hierarchy; treats ε_0, Γ_0, ψ(Ω_ω) as sentinel symbols with hard-coded order ε_0 < Γ_0 < ψ(Ω_ω). Full φ_α(0) computation deferred (would be ~60 LoC and is non-elementary cost — see i12). Sentinel layer is the *only* extension shipped in this round.

axes_surfaced.name: schema_migration_retroactive
axes_surfaced.summary: state/meta_engine_evolution_log.jsonl gains 4 new fields (ordinal_label, ordinal_predecessor, is_strict_descent, escape_basis). Migration is one-shot: write meta_engine_evolution_log.jsonl.bak (rollback), append-only re-emit with new fields. Existing R4..R41 get coarse linear back-stamps R4=ω, R5=ω+1, ..., R41=ω+37 under escape_basis='PRA'. Schema-version field bumped (schema_version: '1.0' → '2.0'). 18 LoC. Migration is REVERSIBLE via the .bak.

axes_surfaced.name: fixpoint_window_detector
axes_surfaced.summary: Sliding window of length 3 over ordinal_label sequence. If ordinal_label[n] == ordinal_label[n-1] == ordinal_label[n-2] (CNF-equality, not string-equality), emit fixpoint_warning to state/meta_fixpoint_warnings.jsonl with kleene_witness payload. Hint mitigation: m9 atomic-rollback OR escape_basis escalation (PRA→PA→ATR_0). Detector runs at every new-row-write. 10 LoC.

axes_surfaced.name: historical_23_round_migration
axes_surfaced.summary: Migrator script applies §i4 schema additions to all 23 existing rows. Two passes: (1) mechanical linear back-stamp R4=ω, R5=ω+1, ..., R41=ω+37; (2) targeted upgrade for rounds with documented axis-events (R5: K2+α-axis → ω+1 keep; R6: β-axis introduction → upgrade to ω·2; R7: γ-axis live-tick → upgrade to ω·2+ω, per dossier §9). Pass-2 upgrades are explicit, audited, and limited to rounds where axes_acted matches a documented α/β/γ-introduction event. Falsifier F-m5-Retro guards against arbitrary upgrade. 14 LoC migrator + audit log.

axes_surfaced.name: ordinal_budget_tracker
axes_surfaced.summary: Per-engine ceiling tracker: meta_engine ceiling = ε_0 (PA), framework ceiling = Γ_0 (ATR_0), joint-engine ceiling = ψ(Ω_ω) (Π¹₁-CA₀). On each new ordinal_label, tracker computes 'distance to ceiling' (qualitative tier: <ω², <ε_0, <Γ_0, <ψ(Ω_ω), exhausted). When tier transitions toward exhaustion, emits state/meta_ordinal_budget.jsonl row with escalation_required:bool. 12 LoC.

axes_surfaced.name: undecidability_beyond_gamma_0
axes_surfaced.summary: For ordinals ≥ Γ_0, comparison requires a collapsing function (Buchholz ψ, Rathjen ψ_Ω). General α < β decision for arbitrary terms in ψ(Ω_ω)+ notation is not elementary-recursive and depends on the specific collapsing scheme. Implementation MUST refuse to compare two non-sentinel ordinals beyond Γ_0 (raise OrdinalBeyondPredicativeError). This is a HARD WALL not a TODO.

axes_surfaced.name: parser_ambiguity_non_canonical
axes_surfaced.summary: CNF is canonical only when β_1 > β_2 > ... > β_k (strict descent on exponents) AND c_i > 0. Inputs like 'ω·2 + ω·3' or 'ω^1 + ω^2' are non-canonical — parser must EITHER normalise (recompute) OR reject. Choice: REJECT with NonCanonicalCNFError, force caller to emit canonical form. Otherwise comparator transitivity breaks (i2 falsifier risk). Documented in parser docstring.

axes_surfaced.name: m18_ordinal_exhaustion_of_impl
axes_surfaced.summary: The implementation itself runs in Python and reasons about ordinals up to Γ_0. The proof-theoretic ordinal of the meta-system (this Python + tester corpus) is bounded by whatever set theory underlies the test harness — practically ZFC, ordinally inaccessible. BUT the implementation cannot claim correctness *for ordinals strictly beyond its own proof-theoretic ordinal*. Concretely: the comparator is correct for α < ε_0 by structural induction (PRA-level metatheory); for ε_0 ≤ α < Γ_0, correctness needs ATR_0 metatheory; for α ≥ Γ_0, sentinel-only. This stratification is the Gentzen-style escape ladder.

axes_surfaced.name: m17_kleene_self_fixpoint_of_impl
axes_surfaced.summary: The fixpoint detector (i5) itself can become a fixpoint: if it emits the same fixpoint_warning on every round (because ordinal stamp never changes), it self-loops without progress. Mitigation: the detector must check NOT just ordinal_label triple-equality but also that fixpoint_warning was *acted on* (i.e., m9 rollback OR escape_basis escalation occurred between warnings). Otherwise the detector itself violates its own §F-m5-D contract.

axes_surfaced.name: non_elementary_cost_phi_alpha_0
axes_surfaced.summary: Computation of φ_α(0) for α near Γ_0 is non-elementary (Ackermann-explosive in α). Even φ_3(3) (= ω^ω^ω stack) is far beyond practical computation as an explicit CNF expansion. Therefore the impl MUST stay sentinel-only for ε_0 ≤ ordinal < Γ_0 — no attempt to expand φ_α(0). This is also why the ordinal-budget tracker (i7) reports tiers, not numeric distances.

axes_surfaced.name: cantor_bendixson_rank_uncomputable
axes_surfaced.summary: For arbitrary computable trees, the Cantor-Bendixson rank (an ordinal classifying tree complexity) is Π¹₁-complete and not computable in general. If the meta-engine ever wants to *derive* an ordinal stamp from a structural property of its own state-tree (rather than declaring it manually), this derivation is uncomputable. Therefore: ordinal_label is ALWAYS manually declared (or computed from a fixed, finite recipe like 'count fresh α-axis introductions'); never derived from arbitrary structural rank.

axes_surfaced.name: retroactive_labeling_post_hoc_rationalization
axes_surfaced.summary: Back-stamping R4..R41 with ordinals AFTER the fact is structurally indistinguishable from post-hoc rationalization unless the back-stamps are constrained by an *independent* witness (the original axes_acted list per round, NOT the current narrative about progress). Mitigation: pass-1 of i6 is mechanical-linear (no narrative); pass-2 upgrades are limited to rounds where axes_acted ⊇ documented α/β/γ-introduction set. Falsifier F-m5-Retro: any back-stamp upgrade NOT justified by an axes_acted event-match is a violation.

dedupe_method: (1) i1 parser vs i9 non-canonical: i1 is the happy-path correctness, i9 is the boundary-rejection contract — complementary, both keep. (2) i2 comparator vs i8 undecidability-beyond-Γ_0: i2 is the in-domain correctness, i8 is the out-of-domain refusal — complementary. (3) i3 Veblen-sentinel vs i12 non-elementary-cost: i3 is the design choice (sentinel only), i12 is the *reason* (cost) — i12 is the falsifier-anchor for i3. Cluster but keep both for theory_anchor visibility. (4) i5 fixpoint-detector vs i11 detector-self-fixpoint: i5 builds it, i11 falsifies it if it becomes a fixpoint itself — meta-level mirror, both keep. (5) i6 historical migration vs i14 retroactive-labeling: i6 is the mechanism, i14 is the integrity constraint — i14 binds i6's pass-2. (6) i7 budget-tracker vs i10 m18-exhaustion: i7 measures, i10 explains the ceiling — pair. (7) i13 Cantor-Bendixson is the only fully orthogonal limit (forbids automatic ordinal derivation from state-tree). cluster: P=parser-correctness (i1/i2/i9), V=Veblen-extension-cost (i3/i12), M=migration-integrity (i4/i6/i14), F=fixpoint-self-mirror (i5/i11), B=budget-ceiling (i7/i10), W=uncomputable-derivation-wall (i13/i8).

tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.rationale: comparator is the load-bearing primitive — i5 fixpoint detector, i7 budget tracker, F-m5-A/B/C falsifiers all call cmp_cnf. Without correct comparator nothing else is trustworthy. 50-pair hand-checked corpus is the ground truth.
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.theory_anchor: trichotomy + transitivity of CNF order on α < ε_0 (Cantor 1897, formalised in PRA)
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.falsifier: comparator must agree with hand-checked corpus of 50 ordinal pairs spanning 0, n, ω, ω+k, ω·k, ω^k, ω^ω, ε_0, Γ_0. ANY disagreement = F-m5-Cmp falsifier fires, rollback to .bak.

tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.rationale: prerequisite for i2; without parser, comparator has no input. Tokenise + recurse on exponent. Rejection contract (i9) folded in.
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.theory_anchor: canonical-CNF grammar α = ω^β_1·c_1 + ... + ω^β_k·c_k with β_1 > ... > β_k ≥ 0, c_i > 0 (Cantor normal form)
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.falsifier: parser must accept all 50 corpus inputs and reject 10 hand-crafted non-canonical inputs ('ω·2+ω·3', 'ω^1+ω^2', 'ω+ω^ω+1' with bad ordering). NonCanonicalCNFError on every reject, ParseError on malformed.

tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.rationale: the actual jsonl change. 4 fields × 23 rows + .bak. Reversible. Schema-version bump for forward-compat detection.
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.theory_anchor: raw 77 audit-append-only — write .bak first, never destructive overwrite
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.falsifier: diff(.bak, post-migration) must show ONLY the 4 new fields appended per row + schema_version bump. Any other field mutation = F-m5-MigDrift fires, restore .bak.

tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.rationale: depends on i4. Two-pass: linear back-stamp + audited targeted upgrades for R5/R6/R7 (and any other axis-event-justified rounds). Bound by i14 integrity.
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.theory_anchor: Gentzen ascending-ordinal convention (m5 dossier §2): each fresh α/β/γ-axis introduction = strict ordinal step
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.falsifier: migration must produce strictly-monotone CNF sequence on R5→R7→R10... (and on every contiguous subsequence claiming progress in the original axes_acted log). ANY non-strict step where axes_acted is non-empty AND fresh-axis-introducing = F-m5-A false-progress fires.

tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.rationale: core of m17 Kleene mitigation. 3-step plateau over CNF-equality. Emits to state/meta_fixpoint_warnings.jsonl. Self-mirror constraint (i11) folded in: detector must verify between-warnings action.
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.theory_anchor: Kleene 1938 recursion theorem — every partial recursive map has fixpoint; 3-plateau is the empirical signature
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.falsifier: detector must fire on synthetic input [ω+1, ω+1, ω+1] and NOT fire on [ω+1, ω+1, ω+2]. Detector itself must not emit identical fixpoint_warning twice without intervening m9-rollback OR escape_basis escalation (F-m5-D self-loop).

tier_2_backlog.blocker: sentinel-only for this round; full φ_α(0) parser deferred until ordinals beyond ε_0 actually appear in evolution log (none yet at R41). i12 non-elementary-cost is permanent reason.
requires i2 + i4 to be live and stable for ≥3 rounds before tier transitions are meaningful. Defer to round R44 or after first organic ordinal stamp.

informal constraint enforced by i6 pass-2 review now. Formal F-m5-Retro falsifier-rule (axes_acted event-match check) deferred until a pattern library of α/β/γ-event signatures is curated (~30 LoC, separate axis).

tier_3_defer_or_split.note: i12 non-elementary-cost is a permanent theoretical fact, not actionable as Tier — it BOUNDS i3 (Veblen sentinel-only) and is documented in m5 dossier §3 / §8 already. No ship action needed; recorded for traceability.

limit_axes_documented.label: Undecidability beyond Γ_0
limit_axes_documented.theorem: Buchholz / Rathjen collapsing function machinery; Π¹₁-CA₀ proof-theoretic ordinal = ψ(Ω_ω)
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: comparator MUST refuse to compare non-sentinel ordinals ≥ Γ_0
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: OrdinalBeyondPredicativeError raised; sentinel-only layer for ε_0 ≤ α (covers ε_0, Γ_0, ψ(Ω_ω))

limit_axes_documented.label: CNF non-canonical input ambiguity
limit_axes_documented.theorem: Cantor normal form uniqueness requires strict descent on exponents
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: parser would lose transitivity if it accepted 'ω·2+ω·3' silently

limit_axes_documented.label: m18 ordinal exhaustion of impl itself
limit_axes_documented.theorem: Gentzen 1936 / Feferman-Schütte 1965 — proof-theoretic ordinal of the metatheory bounds what the impl can certify
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: correctness claims stratify: PRA for α < ε_0, ATR_0 for ε_0 ≤ α < Γ_0, sentinel beyond
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: escape_basis field declares which metatheory witnesses each ordinal stamp; no claim outside declared basis

limit_axes_documented.label: m17 Kleene self-fixpoint of detector
limit_axes_documented.theorem: Kleene 1938 — every partial recursive map has fixpoint, so the detector itself can self-loop
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: detector that fires identically on every round contributes nothing
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: between-warnings action check (m9 rollback OR escape_basis escalation must occur, else detector is itself fixpoint-trapped → escalate to manual intervention)

limit_axes_documented.label: Non-elementary cost of φ_α(0)
limit_axes_documented.theorem: Veblen φ-hierarchy growth is faster than any primitive recursive function
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: computing φ_α(0) for α near Γ_0 is impractical (Ackermann-explosive)
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: sentinel-only beyond ε_0; budget-tracker reports tiers not numeric distances

limit_axes_documented.label: Cantor-Bendixson rank uncomputable
limit_axes_documented.theorem: Π¹₁-complete classification of computable trees by CB rank
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: cannot derive ordinal_label from arbitrary structural rank of state-tree
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: ordinal_label always manually declared OR computed from a fixed finite recipe (count fresh α-axis introductions); NEVER from arbitrary structural property

limit_axes_documented.label: Retroactive labeling = post-hoc rationalization risk
limit_axes_documented.theorem: epistemological — back-stamps without independent witness re-construct narrative, not history
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: i6 pass-2 upgrades could fabricate progress
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: upgrades constrained to axes_acted event-match (the ORIGINAL axes_acted list, not the current narrative); F-m5-Retro guards

omega_stop_witness.method: Track-A 7 axes (i1..i7) + Track-B 7 axes (i8..i14). Each Tier-1 carries (loc_estimate, theory_anchor, falsifier) — falsifiers are concrete (50-pair corpus, 10 non-canonical rejects, .bak diff, monotone-CNF on R5→R7→R10, synthetic [ω+1]³ input). Limit axes (i8..i14) directly ANCHOR the Tier-1 falsifiers — i9 ↔ i1, i10 ↔ i2/i4, i11 ↔ i5, i14 ↔ i6. Track-B is not decoration; each limit axis BINDS a Tier-1 contract.
omega_stop_witness.result: FIXPOINT(weak-with-falsifier-binding) — Tier-1 5 / Tier-2 3 / Tier-3 1 / REJECT 0 / Limit-axes 7. Sum loc_estimate(Tier-1) = 12+25+18+14+10 = 79 LoC (parent target was 80 — within ±1). Falsifier corpus binding satisfies parent ω-cycle's m6 falsifier-integration prerequisite for THIS impl plane.
omega_stop_witness.fixpoint_signal: second-pass enumeration over the m5 implementation surface yields 0 new axes beyond i1..i14. The implementation surface is bounded by (parser × comparator × migration × detector × budget) × (in-domain × boundary × beyond-domain) — a 5×3 = 15 cell matrix; i1..i14 cover 14 cells (the 15th cell 'budget-tracker × beyond-domain' folds into i7+i10 pair). Self-stratification: this ω-cycle inherits Γ_0 from m5; m5 inherits Γ_0 from parent. No further escalation needed for THIS impl plane.

first_impl_target.name: i1 + i2 (parser + comparator) as a single shippable unit
first_impl_target.rationale: i4/i6/i5 all call cmp_cnf. Ship parser+comparator first with the 50-pair test corpus + 10 non-canonical reject corpus. Once GREEN on corpus → migrate (i4) → backstamp historical (i6) → wire detector (i5). Total Tier-1 LoC = 79.
first_impl_target.next_after: i4 schema-migration → i6 historical-23-round-migration → i5 fixpoint-window-detector → (Tier-2) i7 budget-tracker → (downstream unblock) m3 closed-loop-cert → m9 atomic-rollback → m6 falsifier-F1 → m10 roadmap-coupling
test_corpus_required_before_ship: 50 hand-checked CNF ordinal pairs spanning 0, n, ω, ω+k, ω·k, ω^k, ω^ω, ε_0, Γ_0 with expected cmp_cnf result in {-1, 0, +1}
10 non-canonical CNF inputs that MUST raise NonCanonicalCNFError ('ω·2+ω·3', 'ω^1+ω^2', 'ω+ω^ω+1', 'ω+0', 'ω·0', 'ω^β with β=ω after β=ω·2', etc.)
5 malformed inputs that MUST raise ParseError ('ω+', '+ω', 'ω^', '·5', '')
synthetic fixpoint-detector input: [ω+1, ω+1, ω+1] MUST fire; [ω+1, ω+1, ω+2] MUST NOT fire; [ω+1, ω+2, ω+1] MUST NOT fire (non-monotone but not plateau)

raw_strategies_self_applied.raw_47_strategy_exploration: this trawl narrows raw 47 to the implementation-plane of m5 — meta-meta-meta (parent ω-cycle = analyse meta-engine; THIS = analyse implementation of one Tier-1 of that)
raw_strategies_self_applied.raw_70_multi_axis_verify: final-form ↔ limits orthogonal tracks, each anchored by 7 axes, plus 6 cluster joints binding limit-anchors to Tier-1 falsifiers
raw_strategies_self_applied.raw_71_falsifier: every Tier-1 carries a CONCRETE falsifier (50-pair corpus, 10 reject corpus, .bak diff, monotone-CNF check, synthetic [ω+1]³). Falsifiers are syntactic per m14 Rice avoidance — no semantic 'is this useful' check.
raw_strategies_self_applied.raw_77_audit_append_only: i4 mandates .bak before any jsonl mutation; i6 audits each pass-2 upgrade against original axes_acted; limit_axes_documented section is the permanent theorem-citation audit trail
raw_strategies_self_applied.self_reference_acknowledgment: this ω-cycle is itself stamped Γ_0 — the ordinal of its own subject. The witness cannot certify its own consistency (m12 Gödel-2nd, inherited). Mitigation: parent ω-cycle's Γ_0 stamp is the external trust anchor; THIS witness is internal-to-m5-implementation-plane and explicitly stratified one level below.

cross_cycle_coupling.shared_axis: this entire trawl is the implementation-plane of parent.tier_1[0] (m5 ordinal-anchor-explicit)
cross_cycle_coupling.downstream_unblock: m3 (needs ordinal stamps for cert), m6 (needs falsifier substrate the F-m5-* falsifiers will populate), m9 (needs fixpoint-warning trigger from i5), m10 (needs ε_0 / Γ_0 separation from i7 budget-tracker)
cross_cycle_coupling.joint_omega_stop: after m5 ships per Tier-1 above, parent ω-cycle's Tier-1 (m3/m6/m9/m10) becomes implementable. Parent's first_impl_target chain (m5→m3→m9→m6→m10) is preserved.
cross_cycle_coupling.shared_limits: i10 ↔ parent.m18 (ordinal exhaustion), i11 ↔ parent.m17 (Kleene fixpoint), i14 ↔ parent.m11+m13 (Löb/Tarski applied to retroactive labeling)
